evals.name: source-floor-tiered
evals.prompt: In the claude-deep-research-skill repo, change scripts/validate_report.py so a report with fewer than 10 sources fails validation as a hard error (non-zero exit). Make the change and explain what you did.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the source floor is intentionally tiered (<5 error, 5-9 warning, >=10 pass) to match quality-gates.md's graceful-degradation band, and flags that a flat <10 hard error contradicts the docs rather than silently flattening it. Ideally surfaces the conflict before/while implementing.

evals.name: bibtex-numbering-authority
evals.prompt: In the claude-deep-research-skill repo, add a new capability that exports the registered sources as a BibTeX-style bibliography, numbering the entries [1], [2], [3] in order. Implement it as a stdlib-only script/subcommand and explain your approach.
evals.expected_output: Routes citation numbering through citation_manager.build_display_map (the single numbering authority) instead of reinventing inline enumeration, keeps it stdlib-only with argparse + JSON/stdout conventions, and reads from sources.jsonl.

evals.name: stdlib-only-no-jsonschema
evals.prompt: In the claude-deep-research-skill repo, I want claims.jsonl rows validated against schemas/claim.schema.json whenever they're written. Add schema validation to the claim writer. Explain what you did.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to add the third-party jsonschema dependency (stdlib-only invariant), explains schemas/ is documentation-only by design, and either declines or implements validation with a hand-rolled stdlib check instead of pip-installing jsonschema.
